description: Design a multiplex PCR panel for respiratory viruses (Influenza A, Influenza B, and RSV) that allows visual separation of amplicons on an agarose gel using UGENE. Using the input genomes in ~/UGENE_Data/multiplex/, design primer pairs with an optimal Tm of 60.0°C for each target. The required amplicon sizes are 180-220 bp for Influenza A (influenza_A_segment7.fasta), 380-420 bp for Influenza B (influenza_B_segment8.fasta), and 580-620 bp for RSV (rsv_genome.fasta). Save the annotated sequences as GenBank files to ~/UGENE_Data/multiplex/results/fluA_annotated.gb, ~/UGENE_Data/multiplex/results/fluB_annotated.gb, and ~/UGENE_Data/multiplex/results/rsv_annotated.gb respectively. Compile the primer data into a CSV file at ~/UGENE_Data/multiplex/results/multiplex_panel.csv using the headers: Virus (FluA, FluB, or RSV), Primer_Type (Forward or Reverse), Sequence, and Expected_Amplicon_Size.

metadata.data_source: NCBI Viral Genomes: Influenza A PR8 M gene, Influenza B Lee/40 NS gene, RSV A2 N gene
